author: vladimir_ea
content: Is this really the design? Builders must subscribe to all attestation subnets in order to determine when they can release the payload? What about the legendary ‘unsophisticated builder’?

author: potuz
content: As soon as you see a block with your bid, releasing the Payload immediately is safe from same slot unbundling because the attestation deadline is passed so even an equivocation should be reorged. There are attacks that may still be successful but not same slot unbundling at least

author: vladimir_ea
content: "same slot" but not across slots then

author: potuz
content: No, attacks are several. One is the proposer reveals a block late or an equivocation and it gets reorged  (or the equivocation included) and then in the next slot it can reuse the Payload's txs. A builder that is worried about this will wait to see enough attestations for the block before revealing

author: potuz
content: The proposer will send the block to the builder so the builder has a good hint on timelines

author: vladimir_ea
content: does that api exist? I thought it was removed and the proposer circulates the beacon block only via p2p. Or is that wrong?

author: potuz
content: Weird if that PR was merged, it makes sense for the proposer to contact the builder for this since they are typically better connected for block broadcasting
